lines.original: And if by chance an honest man like yourself should make enemies
lines.translated: And if, by chance, an honest man like you makes enemies

lines.original: Then, they would become my enemies
lines.translated: Then they would become my enemies

lines.original: And then they would fear you (fear you)
lines.translated: And then they would fear you (fear you)

lines.original: डब्बी भरे फिराँ गाडी जमाँ काळी ले के (काळी ले के)
lines.translated: I go around with boxes full, taking a black car (black one)
lines.transliteration: Dabbi bhare phirān gāḍī jamāṁ kālī le ke (kālī le ke)
lines.note: डब्बी (dabbi) = crates/boxes; काली/काळी (kālī) = black. The exact function of 'जमाँ / जमां' here is unclear in dialect — likely linking to 'with/like'.

lines.original: घुमाँ सर-ए-आम बंदे पूरे चाळी ले के (चाळी)
lines.translated: I parade openly with a full crew of forty (forty)
lines.transliteration: Ghumāṁ sar-e-ām bande pūre cāḷī le ke (cāḷī)
lines.note: सर-ए-आम = in public. चाळी / cāḷī is likely '40' (forty), i.e., a group of forty people or a crew.

lines.original: चक्या पूरा जिम्मा वैरियाँ की funeral का
lines.translated: I took full responsibility for the enemies' funeral
lines.transliteration: Chakyā pūrā zimma vairiyāṅ kī funeral kā
lines.note: वैरियाँ (vairiyāṅ) = enemies. 'चक्या' (chakya) appears to be dialectal for 'handled/took care of/took on'.

lines.original: ख़ुद पहुँचाँ कराँ कफ़नों पराळी ले के
lines.translated: I'll come myself with shrouds and straw
lines.transliteration: Kẖud pahunchāṅ karāṅ kafanõ parāḷī le ke
lines.note: कफ़नों (kafanõ) = shrouds; पराळी (parāḷī) = straw/stubble (often used for funeral pyres in rural contexts).

lines.original: मो-मो-मोर बत्ती लागी, ३६ घूमें देश, छोरी
lines.translated: Mo‑mo‑mor light lit; thirty‑six toured the country, girl
lines.transliteration: Mo-mo-mor baṭṭī lāgī, 36 ghoomeṅ deś, chhorī
lines.note: मो‑मो‑मोर appears onomatopoeic or highly colloquial/slang; छोरी (chhorī) = girl. '३६ घूमें देश' literally reads as '36 toured the country' but exact cultural reference is unclear.

lines.original: पेश होए नहीं आँ, बड़े करैं chase, छोरी
lines.translated: They don't show up; they do a big chase, girl.
lines.transliteration: pesh hoye nahin aan, bade karen chase, chhori

lines.original: सीख्या India में जादू यो Bengal आळा
lines.translated: I learned that Bengal-style magic in India.
lines.transliteration: sikhya India mein jadu yo Bengal aala
lines.note: Here 'आळा / aala' indicates 'from' or 'of that type' (Bengal-style / of the Bengal person).

lines.original: गंगा तार देवाँ बदळ के भेस, छोरी
lines.translated: I'll cross the Ganges after changing my disguise, girl.
lines.transliteration: ganga taar devaan badal ke bhes, chhori
lines.note: Minor uncertainty over 'तार/taar' — read here as 'cross (the Ganges)'.

lines.original: ठेका parking का ले रहे आँ कचहरी आळा (कचहरी आळा)
lines.translated: Court people are taking the bar's parking spot (kacheri people).
lines.transliteration: theka parking ka le rahe aan kacheri aala (kacheri aala)
lines.note: ठेका (theka) commonly refers to a liquor shop/bar; कचहरी (kacheri) refers to court/officials.

lines.original: एक case चालै HAU में firing आळा (firing आळा)
lines.translated: There's a case going on; there's firing at HAU.
lines.transliteration: ek case chale HAU mein firing aala (firing aala)
lines.note: HAU is likely a place/institution (abbreviation); 'firing aala' means someone who fires/shoots or an incident of firing.

lines.original: ठेके L1 की दारू आळे जाणैं सारे
lines.translated: Everyone who supplies L1 liquor knows.
lines.transliteration: theke L1 ki daru aale jaanen saare
lines.note: L1 likely refers to a liquor license/classification; 'दारू आळे' = people dealing with alcohol.

lines.original: जाणै कल्ले-कल्ले शहर के मैं थाणे, बिल्लो
lines.translated: They know every police station in every city, girl.
lines.transliteration: jaane kalle-kalle shahar ke main thane, billo
lines.note: थाणा (thane) = police station; बिल्लो / billo is an affectionate address to a woman.

lines.original: ले के Russia के मुँहाँ पे बंडाने, बिल्लो (बंडाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: Taking bandanas to Russia's face, girl.
lines.transliteration: le ke Russia ke muh pe bandane, billo (bandane, billo)
lines.note: बंडाने = bandanas; 'मुँहाँ पे बंडाने' means wearing/placing bandanas on the face. 'Russia' likely refers to the country or a named place/person.

lines.original: चूके कदे वी नहींं मारे होए निसाने, बिल्लो (निसाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: They never miss their mark, billo.
lines.transliteration: Chuke kade vi nahin maare hoye nisaane, billo (nisaane, billo)
lines.note: बिल्लो (billo) is an affectionate vocative for a woman (see glossary entry 'biba'/'beeba').

lines.original: टंग्या खब्बे पासे butt जो सुनहरे आळा
lines.translated: A golden butt hangs on the left side.
lines.transliteration: Tangya khabbe paase butt jo sunahre aala
lines.note: Butt is English here; could refer to a gun butt or to buttocks—see ambiguity.

lines.original: गोळ थूकणे के टोंदा फ़िरे बहाने, बिल्लो (बहाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: He spits bullets and wanders around making excuses, billo.
lines.transliteration: Gol thukne ke tonda phire bahane, billo (bahane, billo)
lines.note: गोळ (gol) can mean 'bullet' or 'round'; थूकणे (thukne) = 'to spit' — imagery may imply shooting or aggressive talk.

lines.original: छोरे reckless, मुँहाँ पे बंडाने, बिल्लो (बंडाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: Boys are reckless, bandanas over their faces, billo.
lines.transliteration: Chhore reckless, munha pe bandane, billo (bandane, billo)
lines.note: मुँहाँ पे बंडाने = bandanas on/over the mouth/face.

lines.original: चूके कदे वी नहींं मारे होए निसाने, बिल्लो (निसाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: They never miss their mark, billo.
lines.transliteration: Chuke kade vi nahin maare hoye nisaane, billo (nisaane, billo)
lines.note: बिल्लो (billo) is an affectionate vocative for a woman (see glossary entry 'biba'/'beeba').

lines.original: टंग्या खब्बे पासे butt जो सुनहरे आळा
lines.translated: A golden butt hangs on the left side.
lines.transliteration: Tangya khabbe paase butt jo sunahre aala
lines.note: Butt is English here; could refer to a gun butt or to buttocks—see ambiguity.

lines.original: गोळ थूकणे के टोंदा फ़िरे बहाने, बिल्लो (बहाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: He spits bullets and wanders around making excuses, billo.
lines.transliteration: Gol thukne ke tonda phire bahane, billo (bahane, billo)
lines.note: गोळ (gol) can mean 'bullet' or 'round'; थुकणे (thukne) = 'to spit' — imagery may imply shooting or aggressive talk.

lines.original: लाके यारी ना गिणाए घाटे-वादे कदे (वादे कदे)
lines.translated: Don't measure friendship by losses and gains, ever.
lines.transliteration: Laake yaari na ginae ghaate-vaade kade (vaade kade)
lines.note: घाटे-वादे refers to profit/loss or give-and-take; line stresses that friendship isn't tallied like accounts.

lines.original: बंद राखी सदा कॉपी मैं हिसाबाँ आळी (हिसाबाँ आळी)
lines.translated: I always keep the notebook closed; I'm the one who keeps the accounts.
lines.transliteration: Band rakhi sada copy main hisaban aali (hisaban aali)

lines.original: ज्यादा ठोकराँ तै मिले ऐं तजरबे रै (तजरबे रै)
lines.translated: I've gained a lot of experience from many knocks.
lines.transliteration: Zyada thokaran te mile ain tajarbe re (tajarbe re)

lines.original: कम मिली मन्ने knowledge किताबाँ आळी
lines.translated: I got little knowledge from books.
lines.transliteration: Kam mili manne knowledge kitaban aali
lines.note: Line mixes English 'knowledge' with Punjabi; literal meaning kept conservative.

lines.original: म्हारी चुप ने ना गिणों कमजोरियाँ में
lines.translated: Don't count my silence as a weakness.
lines.transliteration: Mhari chup ne na gino kamzoriyaan mein

lines.original: खाज मेट देवाँ, वैद आ पुराणे, बिल्लो
lines.translated: I'll cure the itch—like a vaid and the old remedies, billo.
lines.transliteration: Khaj met devaan, vaid aa purane, billo
lines.note: 'billo' is an affectionate vocative (addressing a woman), similar to 'biba' in the provided glossary.

lines.original: ले के Russia के मुँहाँ पे बंडाने, बिल्लो (बंडाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: Putting a bandana on Russia's face, billo.
lines.transliteration: Le ke Russia ke muh pe bandane, billo (bandane, billo)
lines.note: 'billo' = affectionate vocative (see glossary).

lines.original: चूके कदे वी नहींं मारे होए निसाने, बिल्लो (निसाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: I've never missed any target I aimed at, billo.
lines.transliteration: Chuke kade vi nahin maare hoe nisaane, billo (nisaane, billo)
lines.note: 'billo' = affectionate vocative (see glossary).

lines.original: टंग्या खब्बे पासे butt जो सुनहरे आळा
lines.translated: A golden butt hanging on my left side.
lines.transliteration: Tangya khabbe paase butt jo sunehre aala
lines.note: The English loan 'butt' could mean the butt/stock of a firearm or the body ('butt'); 'सुनहरे आळा' is read as 'the golden one.'

lines.original: गोळ थूकणे के टोंदा फ़िरे बहाने, बिल्लो (बहाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: Spitting bullets and wandering around with excuses, billo (excuses, billo)
lines.transliteration: Gol thookne ke tonda phire bahane, billo (bahane, billo)
lines.note: बिल्लो (billo) is an affectionate vocative for a woman; 'गोळ/गोल' likely refers to a 'bullet' and 'थूकणे' ('thookne') literally 'to spit' — here possibly slang for firing. 'टोंदा फ़िरे' is regional/dialectal and read conservatively as 'wanders around'.

lines.original: छोरे reckless, मुँहाँ पे बंडाने, बिल्लो (बंडाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: The boys are reckless, with bandanas over their faces, billo (bandana, billo)
lines.transliteration: Chhore reckless, munhā̃ pe bandana, billo (bandana, billo)

lines.original: चूके कदे वी नहींं मारे होए निसाने, बिल्लो (निसाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: They never miss their marks, billo (targets, billo)
lines.transliteration: Chuke kade vi nahin maare hoye nisaane, billo (nisaane, billo)

lines.original: टंग्या खब्बे पासे butt जो सुनहरे आळा
lines.translated: A golden butt hangs on the left side
lines.transliteration: Tangya khabbe pase butt jo sunahre aala
lines.note: Phrase mixes Punjabi with the English word 'butt'. 'टंग्या' and 'आळा' are dialectal; exact referent of 'butt' (object, person, or slang) is unclear.

lines.original: गोळ थूकणे के टोंदा फ़िरे बहाने, बहाने, बहाने
lines.translated: Spitting bullets, wandering around with excuses — excuses, excuses
lines.transliteration: Gol thookne ke tonda phire bahane, bahane, bahane

lines.original: Liquid सवा लाख का गलासियाँ मैं (गलासियाँ मैं)
lines.translated: Liquid worth one hundred twenty-five thousand in glasses (in glasses)
lines.transliteration: Liquid sawa lakh ka gilaasiyan main (gilaasiyan main)
lines.note: 'सवा लाख' = 125,000 (units implied, likely rupees); 'Liquid' likely refers to expensive liquor; 'गलासियाँ' = 'glasses'.

lines.original: माल पूरा मिल्या परसों तलासियाँ में(तलासियाँ में)
lines.translated: Found all the goods during searches the day before yesterday (during searches)
lines.transliteration: Maal pura milya parson talasiyan mein (talasiyan mein)
lines.note: परसों (parson) can mean 'the day before yesterday' (or sometimes 'the day after tomorrow' in other dialects); read here conservatively as 'the day before yesterday'.

lines.original: Suit करदा नहींं weather, रै, बाहर आळा
lines.translated: A suit doesn't suit the weather, re — the one outside
lines.transliteration: Suit karda nahin weather, re, bahar aala
lines.note: Mixture of English and Punjabi; 'रै' (re) is a colloquial vocative/exclamatory particle and 'आळा' (aala) usually means 'one' or 'person'.

lines.original: हवा भीतर आळी लिखी मेरी रासियाँ में
lines.translated: Written in the air, in my rasiyaan.
lines.transliteration: Hawa bheetar aali likhi meri rasiyaan mein
lines.note: रासियाँ (rasiyaan) likely a regional word (literally 'ropes'/'ties') — meaning is uncertain in context.

lines.original: ते-तेरी चढ़दी जवानी, मेरा पारा, गोरी (पारा, गोरी)
lines.translated: Your rising youth raises my temperature, girl.
lines.transliteration: Te-teri chadhdi jawani, mera paara, gori (paara, gori)

lines.original: करवा के हटैगा कोई कारा, गोरी (कारा, गोरी)
lines.translated: I'll have someone remove any 'kaara', girl.
lines.transliteration: Karwa ke hataega koi kaara, gori (kaara, gori)
lines.note: The word 'कारा' (kaara) is unclear in this line — can mean different things regionally (e.g., an obstacle, a mark, or even 'kada'/bangle in some dialects).

lines.original: चंडीगढ़ seize करैं हरियाणे आळे
lines.translated: The guys from Haryana will seize Chandigarh.
lines.transliteration: Chandigarh seize karan Haryane aale

lines.original: टेढी आँख तै तो कर तू इशारा, गोरी
lines.translated: Give the signal with a sidelong glance, girl.
lines.transliteration: Tedhi aankh te to kar tu ishara, gori

lines.original: तळे Jordan, मोढे Burberry पहराँ (Burberry पहराँ)
lines.translated: I wear Jordans on my feet and Burberry draped on my shoulders.
lines.transliteration: Tale Jordan, modhe Burberry pehraan (Burberry pehraan)
lines.note: Jordan and Burberry are international brand names used here as style markers.

lines.original: जमाँ line मैं लगा के कोढे वैरी कराँ (वैरी कराँ)
lines.translated: I'll line them up and deal with my enemies.
lines.transliteration: Jama line main laga ke kodhe vaeri karaan (vaeri karaan)
lines.note: 'वैरी' (vaeri) = enemy (see glossary). The word 'कोढे' (kodhe) is unclear/colloquial here and could refer to a group, heads, or a local slang term for opponents.

lines.original: वैसे गाम में रवाँ, हाँ, देसी बणके मैं
lines.translated: Anyway I go to my village, yeah, becoming desi.
lines.transliteration: Vaise gaam mein rawan, haan, desi banke main
lines.note: 'देसी' (desi) means local/native/traditional — here 'becoming desi' suggests returning to or adopting a local/native style or attitude.

lines.original: तेरे शहर में drip जमाँ जहरी कराँ
lines.translated: I'll bring toxic drip to your city.
lines.transliteration: Tere shahar mein drip jamaān jahri karān
lines.note: Here 'drip' is English slang for flashy style; 'jahri karān' literally 'make poisonous' likely used metaphorically (intensify/make lethal).

lines.original: छोरा educate, लाइए ना गवाराँ में तै
lines.translated: I'm educated — don't lump me in with the uneducated.
lines.transliteration: Chhora educate, laiye na gawaraan mein tai
lines.note: Chhora = boy; 'gawaraan' (from 'gawar') = uneducated/rustic people.

lines.original: नहीं मिलैं मेरे जिसे, रै, हजाराँ में तै
lines.translated: You won't find someone like me even among thousands.
lines.transliteration: Nahin milain mere jise, re, hazaaran mein tai

lines.original: हीरे-कोयले की खानाँ में ना टोवे, छोरी
lines.translated: You won't be found in diamond-and-coal mines, girl.
lines.transliteration: Heere-koyale ki khaanān mein na tove, chhori
lines.note: Addressing the girl (chhori).

lines.original: तन्ने ढूँढदी ना लग ज्या जमाने, बिल्लो
lines.translated: The world won't start looking for you, billo.
lines.transliteration: Tanne dhoondhdi na lag jya zamaane, billo
lines.note: Billo = affectionate address for a girl (see glossary: biba/beeba).

lines.original: ले के Russia के मुँहाँ पे बंडाने, बिल्लो (बंडाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: Wearing a Russian bandana over your mouth, billo.
lines.transliteration: Le ke Russia ke muh pe bandane, billo (bandane, billo)
lines.note: ‘Bandane/बंडाने’ = bandana; likely literal cloth tied over the face.

lines.original: चूके कदे वी नहींं मारे होए निसाने, बिल्लो (निसाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: I never miss my targets, billo.
lines.transliteration: Chuke kade vi nahin maare hoye nisaane, billo (nisaane, billo)

lines.original: टंग्या खब्बे पासे butt जो सुनहरे आळा
lines.translated: On the left side hangs a golden butt.
lines.transliteration: Tangya khabbe paase butt jo sunahre aala
lines.note: ‘खब्बे पासे’ = on the left side. 'बिल्लो' not in this line but earlier addressed the girl. 'butt' is English here — could mean buttocks, a cigarette butt, or gun butt depending on context.

lines.original: गोळ थूकणे के टोंदा फ़िरे बहाने, बिल्लो (बहाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: He wanders around making excuses like he's spitting bullets, billo (excuses, billo)
lines.transliteration: gol thookne ke tonda phire bahaane, billo (bahaane, billo)
lines.note: ‘billo’ is an affectionate vocative for a woman (similar to 'girl'); kept here as 'billo'.

lines.original: छोरे reckless, मुँहाँ पे बंडाने, बिल्लो (बंडाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: The boys are reckless, bandanas over their mouths, billo
lines.transliteration: chhore reckless, munh pe bandane, billo (bandane, billo)
lines.note: ‘billo’ is an affectionate vocative for a woman; 'bandane' = bandanas. 'chhore' = boys/young men.

lines.original: चूके कदे वी नहींं मारे होए निसाने, बिल्लो (निसाने, बिल्लो)
lines.translated: We've never missed our targets, billo
lines.transliteration: chuke kade vi nahin maare hoye nisaane, billo (nisaane, billo)
lines.note: ‘nisaane’ = targets/aims; phrase expresses not missing a shot or figurative aim.

lines.original: टंग्या खब्बे पासे butt जो सुनहरे आळा
lines.translated: Hanging on the left side, a butt with golden trim
lines.transliteration: tangya khabbe paase butt jo sunahre aala
lines.note: Contains English 'butt' in the original. 'सुनहरे आळा / sunahre aala' likely means 'golden trim/ornament' but 'आळा/aala' is dialectal and ambiguous.

lines.original: गोळ थूकणे के टोंदा फ़िरे बहाने, बिल्लो (बहाने, बहाने)
lines.translated: He wanders around making excuses like he's spitting bullets, billo (excuses, excuses)
lines.transliteration: gol thookne ke tonda phire bahaane, billo (bahaane, bahaane)
lines.note: Repeated line; same ambiguities as the earlier occurrence. 'billo' kept as vocative.
